gazette: Turkish Medicines and Medical Devices Agency
title: Announcement Regarding Update process on Cosmetic Regulation
bodyHtml:
The Turkish Medicines and Medical Devices Agency has announced the initiation of update works on the Cosmetic Products Regulation, prepared in compliance with the Regulation (EC) No 223/2009 of the European Parliament. These updates are being carried out in accordance with the EU Commission Regulation 2024/858 and 2024/996.

On March 15, 2024, Regulation 2024/858 regarding nanomaterials was published in the Official Gazette by the EU Commission. It came into effect on the 20th day after its publication (April 4, 2024).

As a result, 11 nanomaterials have been added to Annex II of the EU Cosmetic Products Regulation, and their use in cosmetics has been prohibited. Additionally, one nanomaterial has been added to Annex III, restricting its use under specific conditions.

The following nanomaterials have been added to Annex II, leading to their prohibition in cosmetics:

Styrene/Acrylates copolymer (nano)

Sodium Styrene/Acrylates copolymer (nano)

Copper (nano), Colloidal Copper (nano)

Colloidal silver (nano)

Gold (nano)

Colloidal Gold (nano)

Gold Thioethylamino Hyaluronic Acid (nano)

Acetyl heptapeptide-9 Colloidal gold (nano)

Platinum (nano)

Colloidal Platinum (nano)

Acetyl tetrapeptide-17 Colloidal Platinum (nano)

Furthermore, the following nanomaterial has been added to Annex III, limiting its use under specific conditions:

Hydroxyapatite (nano): For toothpaste: < %10, For mouthwash: < %0,465. This component should not be used in applications that may expose the end-user to inhalation. Only nanomaterials consisting of uncoated or surface-unmodified rod-shaped particles, of which at least 95.8% (in terms of particle number) have an aspect ratio of < 3 and the remaining 4.2% have an aspect ratio of < 4.9, are allowed.

The deadlines for compliance are as follows: February 1, 2025, for products to be placed on the market for the first time, and November 1, 2025, for existing products already on the market.

On April 4, 2024, the European Union Official Gazette published Commission Regulation (EU) 2024/996. This Regulation includes changes to be made in the Cosmetic Regulation, as indicated by the European Commission in the draft notification submitted to the WTO on June 8, 2023. These changes specifically concern the use of A Vitamin compounds, Arbutin, Alpha-Arbutin, Triclosan, Triclocarban, Kojic Acid, Daidzein, Genistein, and 4-Methylbenzylidene Camphor, and have been made on Annexes II, III, V, and VI of Regulation (EC) No 1223/2009.
